note: Deterministic test double for serve_live.py (D44). It replaces ONLY the model call; verify.py still runs in the loop, so every served item is really verified and the no-fabrication invariant holds. Wired into the isolated e2e runner via MANIFOLD_LIVE_FIXTURES. Display fields (stem/solution/distractor_rationales) use delimited-LaTeX per Task 2 (so they pass serve_live's display-LaTeX gate and render through MathText); the CHOICES stay sympy-ASCII because verify.py parses them. 'default' is a verifiable draft returned for any skill (exercises serve->verify->grade); 'by_skill' can force an unverifiable draft so the abstain path (verify-in-the-loop, no fabrication) is exercised too.

default.stem: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers shown equals \(5\)?
distractor_rationales: \(\frac{1}{12} = \frac{3}{36}\) counts only three favorable ordered pairs, dropping one of the four that sum to \(5\).
\(\frac{1}{6} = \frac{6}{36}\) is the probability the sum is \(7\), the single most likely total, not \(5\).
\(\frac{2}{9} = \frac{8}{36}\) double-counts the four ordered pairs as if there were eight.
\(\frac{5}{36}\) uses five favorable outcomes, the count for a sum of \(6\), not \(5\).
default.solution: There are \(6 \cdot 6 = 36\) equally likely ordered outcomes. Exactly four of them sum to \(5\): \((1,4), (2,3), (3,2), (4,1)\). So \(P(\text{sum} = 5) = \frac{4}{36} = \frac{1}{9}\).

mf_e2e_force_abstain.stem: Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum equals \(5\)?
distractor_rationales: wrong on purpose: this fixture makes the claimed answer 1/10, which the verifier recomputes as false.
mf_e2e_force_abstain.solution: This fixture is deliberately unverifiable: the true probability is \(\frac{1}{9}\), but no listed choice equals \(\frac{1}{9}\), so verify.py rejects every attempt and serve_live abstains (no fabrication).
